title: Post-Process Energy Forge
description: A robot and a corset float in a dark void, connected by an energy beam. The user manipulates a shared 'energy budget' across six post-processing channels (bloom intensity, chromatic aberration, vignette darkness, pixelation, color grading hue-shift, and film grain) distributed between the two objects using selective render passes. Adjustments must obey a conservation law: total energy across all channels is fixed, so boosting one effect on one object forces reduction elsewhere. Full undo/redo history tracks every change.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer from three/addons. Load Corset.glb (centered left) and robot.glb (centered right) using GLTFLoader. Place a PerspectiveCamera at z=4 with OrbitControls. Add ambient light (0.3) and two directional lights tinted cyan and magenta aimed at each model. Render using two separate layers: layer 1 for the corset, layer 2 for the robot. Use two EffectComposers each with their own RenderPass filtering by layer, then combine via a custom ShaderPass that additively blends both. Implement six post-process channels: bloom (UnrealBloomPass strength 0-3), chromatic aberration (custom ShaderPass offset 0-0.05), vignette darkness (ShaderPass 0-1.5), pixelation (ShaderPass pixel size 1-16), hue-shift color grading (ShaderPass 0-360 degrees), and film grain (ShaderPass intensity 0-1). Each channel has TWO sliders (one per object) shown in a side panel, totaling 12 sliders. Enforce a conservation law: the global energy budget is 6.0 units. Each channel's two slider values are normalized to 0-1 and summed across all 12 sliders must equal 6.0. When a user drags any slider up, proportionally reduce all other sliders to maintain the sum, visually highlighting affected sliders in red briefly. Display the current budget as a bar at the top. Add a glowing energy beam (custom tube geometry with emissive pulsing shader) connecting both models whose color interpolates based on which side has more total energy. Implement undo/redo with Ctrl+Z and Ctrl+Y storing full slider state snapshots (minimum 50 levels). Add a 'Randomize' button that randomly redistributes the budget and a 'Reset' button restoring defaults (all sliders at 0.5). The final composite output renders to screen. Ensure smooth 60fps performance.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  corsetBloomEnergy: number,         // bloom intensity allocated to corset (0-1)
  robotBloomEnergy: number,         // bloom intensity allocated to robot (0-1)
  corsetChromaticEnergy: number,         // chromatic aberration allocated to corset (0-1)
  robotChromaticEnergy: number,         // chromatic aberration allocated to robot (0-1)
  corsetVignetteEnergy: number,         // vignette darkness allocated to corset (0-1)
  robotVignetteEnergy: number,         // vignette darkness allocated to robot (0-1)
  corsetPixelationEnergy: number,         // pixelation allocated to corset (0-1)
  robotPixelationEnergy: number,         // pixelation allocated to robot (0-1)
  corsetHueShiftEnergy: number,         // hue-shift color grading allocated to corset (0-1)
  robotHueShiftEnergy: number,         // hue-shift color grading allocated to robot (0-1)
  corsetGrainEnergy: number,         // film grain allocated to corset (0-1)
  robotGrainEnergy: number,         // film grain allocated to robot (0-1)
  totalEnergyBudget: number,         // sum of all 12 energy values, must equal 6.0
  modelsLoaded: boolean,         // true when both Corset.glb and robot.glb are loaded
}
